titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/296

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=347 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:33:17.655186+00 | translation moa=348 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:33:15.903825+00

div.p:
Omnibus hoc scriptum uisuris uel audituris Willelmus de vesci salutem in domino . Noueritis me concessisse et hac presenti
carta mea confirmasse donum quod Walterus filius Alani fecit deo et beate Marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo
seruientibus scilicet de tota terra quam idem .W. de me tenuit in Molle cum omnibus pertinentiis suis sicut carta ipsius
.W. quam inde habent plenius protestatur  habendam et tenendam ipsis monachis in perpetuum . in liberam . puram . et
perpetuam elemosinam . adeo libere et quiete sicut aliqua elemosina liberius uel quiecius imfra regionem scocie dari poterit .
In cuius rei testimonium presens scriptum sigilli mei apposicione roboraui . Hiis testibus . Willelmo de vescy fratre meo .
Iohanne de lexintun’ . Reynero theutonico militibus . Symone de Horseleye . Roberto de Ewrth . Roberto de valle .

p.cl: To all who shall view or hear this writing, William de Vescy sends greeting in the Lord.

to have allowed and by this my present charter to have made firm the gift that Walter son of Alan has made to God and Blessed Mary at Melrose and the monks serving God in that place; that is of the whole land that the same Walter held of me in Mow, with all its pertinents, as the charter of Walter himself which they have thereupon more fully bears witness; for the same monks to have and to hold for ever, in free, pure, and everlasting alms, as freely and quitely as one can be given any alms freely or quitely within the regio of Scotia.
As testimony of this matter I have strengthened the present document by the application of my seal.
With these witnesses: William de Vescy my brother, John of Lexington, Rainer the German, knights; Simon of Horsley, Robert of Airth, Robert de Valle.
